perturbation.original_text: B. A one-time $10,000USD (Ten Thousand Dollars) promotional fee payable to NFLA. The $10,000 promotional fee shall be payable in four (4) quarterly payments beginning in 2018: Q1 ($2500); Q2 ($2500); Q3 ($2500); Q4 ($2500). Each payment shall be delivered by no later than the 10t h day of the first month of each quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: B. A one-time $10,000USD (Ten Thousand Dollars) promotional fee payable to NFLA. The $10,000 promotional fee shall be payable in four (4) quarterly payments beginning in 2018: Q1 ($2500); Q2 ($2500); Q3 ($2500); Q4 ($2500). Each payment shall be delivered by no later than the 20th day of the first month of each quarter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the due date of the quarterly payments from the 10th to the 20th of the month. This contradicts Section Seven, which states that all payments shall be made no later than ten (10) business days after the end of each quarter. The inconsistency creates uncertainty regarding which payment deadline should be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION SEVEN. PAYMENTS All payments shall be made by wire transfer drawn to the account of NFLA-NC no later than ten (10) business days after the end of each quarter as follows: $0.05 per Unit as described herein of Company's Products sold in the Contract Territory payable to NFLA-NC. Donated amounts will be allocated and dispersed to the NFLA-NC beginning on the first full quarter (three month period) of the Agreement and continue on a quarterly basis thereafter for the term of this Agreement. Past due payments under this Agreement shall bear interest at the rate of: (a) 1% per month; or (b) the maximum interest rate permissible under law, whichever is less. All amounts in this section are in United States dollars.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment due date to the 20th day of the first month of each quarter. This directly contradicts Section Seven, which states that payments are due no later than ten (10) business days after the *end* of each quarter. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding payment deadlines, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding the timing of payments.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees that it will continue in good-faith to produce and market Licensed Products in the same manner that it is currently producing and marketing such items as of September 2017, unless Company and the NFLA/NFLA-NC believe it is not commercially reasonable to continue to produce and market the Licensed Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees that it may, at its sole discretion, alter or discontinue the production and marketing of Licensed Products if it deems it necessary for business purposes, notwithstanding any prior agreements regarding the manner of production and marketing. However, the Company will notify the NFLA/NFLA-NC of this decision, with no obligation to obtain approval.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by giving the Company the sole discretion to alter or discontinue product lines. This directly contradicts the original text, which required them to continue producing and marketing Licensed Products in the same manner as of September 2017, unless both parties agreed it wasn't commercially reasonable. This undermines the NFLA/NFLA-NC's expected benefits from the endorsement agreement, impacting their revenue and exposure. It creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty about whether the NFLA-NC has any say in the marketing of the licensed products
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. The NFLA shall list the Company as a sponsor on all of its promotional materials, websites or other electronic media. The NFLA shall allow the Company to participate in local and national NFLA functions (i.e. Super Bowl Parties, Award Ceremonies, Banquets, etc…) at the Company's own expense. E. The NFLA shall promote Company and Company's products to NFLA database and audience by deliverables listed in Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives the Company sole discretion to alter or discontinue product lines, contradicting the original text which required them to continue production/marketing as of September 2017 unless both parties agreed it was not commercially reasonable. This directly impacts the contradicted text, which outlines the NFLA's obligations to promote the Company and its products based on the presumption of continued production and marketing of those products. If the company can unilaterally discontinue products, the NFLA's promotional efforts become uncertain and the value of their deliverables (listed in Exhibit B) is undermined, creating a direct conflict and impacting the benefits the NFLA was expecting from the deal.

perturbation.original_text: All notices or submissions to be made or delivered by the Company, the NFLA or the NFLA-NC pursuant to this Agreement shall be delivered to the agent's address below, free of all charges (for example, shipping charges and customs charges). If any such shipping charges are paid by another party or by its authorized agent, the corresponding party agrees to make prompt reimbursement.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices or submissions to be made or delivered by the Company to the NFLA or the NFLA-NC pursuant to this Agreement shall be delivered to the agent's address below, with all charges, including shipping and custom charges, being the sole responsibility of the receiving party. There shall be no obligation for reimbursement of any charges.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by shifting the responsibility for shipping and customs charges from the sender to the receiver. This directly contradicts the initial clause that states notices should be delivered 'free of all charges' and that the sender will promptly reimburse charges paid by the recipient. The conflicting terms create confusion about which party is financially responsible for these costs, impacting the overall cost of the deal and potentially leading to legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION EIGHT. AUTHORIZED AGENT Each party shall designate its authorized agent for all purposes under this Agreement. All notices or submissions to be made or delivered by the Company, the NFLA or the NFLA-NC pursuant to this Agreement shall be delivered to the agent's address below, with all charges, including shipping and custom charges, being the sole responsibility of the receiving party. There shall be no obligation for reimbursement of any charges. If any such shipping charges are paid by another party or by its authorized agent, the corresponding party agrees to make prompt reimbursement. All notices or submissions to be made or delivered to Company pursuant to this Agreement shall be delivered to: The Company Food For Athletes/Gridiron BioNutrients™ Attention: Darren Long 1147 N Roseburg Ct STE A, Visalia CA, 93291 NFLA-NC National Football League Alumni - Northern California Chapter Attention: Russell Isaacson - Comptroller 1311 Madison Avenue Redwood CA 94061 NFLA National Football League Alumni, Inc. Attention: Elvis Gooden 8000 Midlantic Drive, 130 S. Mount Laurel, NJ. 08054
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the responsibility for shipping and custom charges from the sender to the receiver. The original text stated that 'All notices or submissions...shall be delivered...free of all charges' and provided for reimbursement. The changed text explicitly makes the receiving party responsible for these charges and disclaims any reimbursement obligation. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the agreement, directly contradicting the original arrangement regarding financial responsibility for shipping and customs charges, thus satisfying the conditions for in-text contradiction according to the provided 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
